author: Gk0Wk
description: CPL Server API - Download stats, ratings, comments, and compatibility

{"tiddlers":{"$:/plugins/Gk0Wk/CPL-Server/docs/changelog":{"title":"$:/plugins/Gk0Wk/CPL-Server/docs/changelog","caption":"<$text text={{{ [[$:/language]get[text]removeprefix[$:/languages/]else[en-GB]search[zh]then[更新日志]else[Changelog]] }}} />","subtitle":"<$text text={{{ [[$:/language]get[text]removeprefix[$:/languages/]else[en-GB]search[zh]then[CPL Server 更新日志]else[CPL Server Changelog]] }}} />","tags":"","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! 2026.06.14

* `GET /cpl/download-plugin/:pluginTitle?version=...` 现在会拒绝不安全的版本文件名，避免历史版本路径被 `../` 或路径分隔符绕出插件历史目录
* `fetch-plugins` 历史归档逻辑修复：现在只在拉取到新版本时才把旧版本移入 `plugin-fetched-history/`，不再把当前版本重复归档
* `index.json` 新增 `versions` 字段，客户端可以据此展示版本选择器

! 2026.06.12

* Moderation model changed from pre-approval to block-only:
  ** `POST /cpl/comments/:pluginTitle` now creates comments with `status: approved` by default; only users in `CPL_BLOCKED_GITHUB_IDS` receive `403 Blocked`
  ** `POST /cpl/compatibility/:pluginTitle` now creates compatibility reports with `status: approved` by default; same block-list check
  ** `CommentTiddlerStore.addComment` respects the incoming `status` field and writes approved comments directly to the `approved/` directory
  ** Pending comments are still supported for backward-compat/admin review of existing pending items

! 2026.06.08

* Download stats in-memory cache: `DownloadStatsTiddlerStore` now maintains an in-memory `downloadCount` cache
  ** Built once on cold start from persisted `.tid` files — no re-aggregation on API calls
  ** Incremented synchronously on each download — `updateDownloadStats()` updates both cache and file
  ** New methods: `getTopDownloadCounts(topN)`, `getDownloadCountsFor(titles)`, `getAllDownloadCounts()` — all O(1) map lookups
* Optimized `/cpl/stats` endpoint:
  ** New `?top=N` query parameter — returns only top-N plugins by download count
  ** Existing `?titles=` and no-filter modes now read from in-memory cache (zero disk I/O)
  ** Response no longer includes `downloadsByIp` — only `downloadCount`, `averageRating`, `totalRatings`
* Deleted legacy `getAllStats()` file-scanning loop — replaced by cache-backed implementation

! 2026.06.07

* Tiddler-based storage: comments, ratings, compatibility reports, and download stats all stored as `.tid` files instead of legacy JSON files
  ** Comments split into `pending/` (gitignored) and `approved/` (version-controlled) directories
  ** Download stats use multi-server suffix (`{name}.{serverId}.tid`) for future multi-instance support
  ** Ratings deduplicated by `githubId` on write and aggregated on read to prevent duplicate votes
* Comments Center API: full comment lifecycle management
  ** `POST /cpl/comments/:pluginTitle` — submit comment (auth required)
  ** `GET /cpl/comments/pending` — admin view of pending comments
  ** `POST /cpl/comments/pending/:id/approve|reject` — moderation actions
  ** `DELETE /cpl/comments/pending/:id` — delete comment
  ** `GET /cpl/comments/all-recent` — public list of recently approved comments
* Batch download stats endpoint: `GET /cpl/stats?titles=t1,t2,t3` fetches stats for multiple plugins in a single request
* Flattened API routes: removed redundant `/api/` path segment, `/cpl/api/*` → `/cpl/*`
* OAuth CSRF protection: server-side validation of GitHub OAuth state parameter, rejects mismatched nonce callbacks
* ETag 304 caching: homepage supports `If-None-Match` conditional requests with `writeHead`/`write` wrapping for chunked response compatibility (Firefox fix)
* Nginx reverse proxy + gzip: static asset gzip compression, API route proxying, sensible cache policies
* Startup loading page & build status badge: server-rendered loading page, `/cpl/build-status` endpoint
* Centralized path management: `src/CPLServer/lib/paths.ts` as single source of truth, eliminating scattered `process.cwd()` / `__dirname` usage
* Test data isolation: test mode (`CPL_TEST_MODE=true`) uses isolated `tmp/test-wiki/` directory to prevent production wiki pollution
* Fixed `parseCompatibilityTiddler` missing `title` field causing compatibility report IDs to always be empty
* Fixed test config tiddlers leaking into production builds
* Docker deployment: `Dockerfile` + `docker-compose.yml`, read-only mode baked in, pending comments directory persisted via volume
* Code quality: zero lint and typecheck errors across entire codebase, Prettier formatting applied
"},"$:/plugins/Gk0Wk/CPL-Server/readme":{"title":"$:/plugins/Gk0Wk/CPL-Server/readme","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! CPL Server

This plugin provides the server-side API used by the TiddlyWiki CPL website and client plugin.

!! Features

* Download statistics with throttling
* Rating submission and aggregation
* Changelog lookup for plugin payloads
* Comment listing, submission, and moderation
* Compatibility report submission and review
* GitHub OAuth based authentication for moderation flows

!! Deploying The Server

# Clone this repository.
# Run `pnpm install` in the repo root.
# Create a `.env` file with the required secrets and admin settings.
# Start the server with either `pnpm server:test` or `pnpm server:prod`.

`pnpm server:test` starts the writable runtime server and is the best local smoke test for the production boot path.

`pnpm server:prod` starts the same runtime in read-only mode and is the safer default for a public deployment.

For development, `pnpm dev` is also a valid way to work on the project because `tiddlywiki-plugin-dev` loads plugins from `src/` automatically. It is useful for interactive development, but it is still a different startup path from the production-like server launcher.

You do ''not'' need to run `pnpm build` before starting the Node.js server. `scripts/server.ts` compiles the runtime plugin payloads automatically and writes them to `cache/runtime-plugins/` before TiddlyWiki starts.

If you want to inspect or edit the wiki content locally, you can open the cloned repository directly in TidGi Desktop because the repo already contains `tidgi.config.json`.

That is useful for editing and reviewing content, but it is ''not'' the same thing as starting the CPL server runtime.

Opening only the `wiki/` folder in TidGi Desktop does not automatically compile `src/CPLServer` and `src/CPLPlugin` into runtime plugins, and it does not recreate the boot arguments injected by `scripts/server.ts`.

Opening the repository root in TidGi Desktop is closer, because the root `tiddlywiki.info` references the CPL plugins. However, that still depends on generated plugin tiddlers already existing locally. It is not the reliable clean-clone bootstrap path for the server API.

If you need the `/cpl/*` routes to work correctly, use `pnpm server:test`, `pnpm server:prod`, or an equivalent custom launcher that performs the same runtime-plugin build and boot-plugin injection.

!! Environment Variables

Required for a real deployment:

* `CPL_JWT_SECRET`
* `CPL_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID`
* `CPL_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET`
* `CPL_ADMIN_GITHUB_IDS`

`CPL_JWT_SECRET` is not provided by GitHub. It is the local server secret used to sign CPL session JWTs after GitHub OAuth identifies the user. Generate it with a command such as `node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(64).toString('hex'))"` and keep it out of git.

Create `CPL_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID` and `CPL_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET` from a GitHub OAuth App. For the public CPL mirror, set the callback URL to `https://cpl.tidgi.fun/cpl/auth/github/callback`.

`CPL_ADMIN_GITHUB_IDS` contains numeric GitHub user IDs, not usernames. You can get one with `curl https://api.github.com/users/<login>` and copy the `id` field, or with `gh api users/<login> --jq .id`.

Optional but useful:

* `CPL_SERVER_ID` for multi-server mirror deployments
* `CPL_JWT_EXPIRY_DAYS`
* `CPL_COMMENT_RATE_LIMIT`
* `CPL_BLOCKED_GITHUB_IDS` for comma-separated numeric GitHub user IDs that may not submit ratings, comments, or compatibility reports
* `HOST`
* `PORT`

!! Authentication Model

Public read APIs do not require a user session. Download counting is intentionally anonymous and uses client IP throttling, because it is a trend signal rather than an identity-bound action.

Ratings, comment submission, and compatibility report submission require GitHub identity. Moderation endpoints additionally require the user's numeric GitHub ID to be listed in `CPL_ADMIN_GITHUB_IDS`.

Login uses GitHub OAuth only to identify the user. After GitHub returns the profile, the CPL server signs its own JWT with `CPL_JWT_SECRET` and sends it to the browser as the `cpl_jwt_token` HttpOnly cookie. Client code cannot read this cookie, and authenticated API requests send it automatically. The browser no longer needs `localStorage` for CPL login state.

Ratings are stored by GitHub ID, so one GitHub account has one rating per plugin and submitting again updates the previous rating.

!! Operational Scripts

* `pnpm server:test` starts the writable local runtime server.
* `pnpm server:prod` starts the read-only server for production-style hosting.
* `pnpm run fetch-plugins` refreshes generated files in `wiki/files/plugin-fetched/`.
* `pnpm run fetch-plugins:dry-run -- --allow-ci --best-effort` previews plugin refreshes without writing files.
* `pnpm run reconcile-data` reports stale or conflicting multi-server data files.
* `pnpm run reconcile-data -- --fix` applies reconciliation fixes.
* `pnpm run reconcile-data -- --clean-stale` removes stale mirror files after the configured grace period.

The static website and library builders are separate concerns:

* `pnpm build`
* `pnpm run build:static-site`
* `pnpm run build:static-library`

Run those in CI or release jobs when you publish the website or library artifacts. They are not required just to run the server.

If the server sits behind a reverse proxy or CDN, make sure the real client IP is forwarded correctly. Throttling and one-vote style protections depend on the client address seen by the CPL server.

!! Suggested Scheduled Tasks

For a public mirror that serves fresh plugin payloads:

* Schedule `pnpm run fetch-plugins` at a regular interval such as hourly or daily.

For multi-server data replication:

* Schedule `pnpm run reconcile-data` as a dry run.
* Run `pnpm run reconcile-data -- --fix` only in a controlled job or after reviewing the report.

For operations hygiene:

* Back up `data/` on a schedule.
* Back up `.env` securely outside git.

!! Runtime Data To Keep

`data/` is the authoritative runtime state. Keep it and back it up.

Examples:

* `data/stats*.json`
* `data/ratings*.json`
* `data/comments/*.json`
* `data/compatibility/*.json`

If you intentionally use git to synchronize multiple writable mirrors, commit `data/` deliberately as part of that workflow. Do not treat it like throwaway cache data.

!! Generated Files To Ignore

These files are generated, reproducible, or disposable. Do not treat them as canonical backups, and do not commit them unless you have a very specific release reason:

* `.env` and `.env.test` must never be committed
* `cache/`
* `dist/`
* `tmp/`
* `playwright-report/`
* `test-results/`
* `wiki/files/plugin-fetched/`
* `wiki/tiddlers/$__plugins_*`
* `wiki/tiddlers/website/`
* logs such as `*.log`

In short: back up `data/` and your secret configuration, but do not back up build caches, generated runtime plugins, test artifacts, or fetched plugin mirrors as if they were primary source data.

!! API Endpoints

* POST /cpl/download/:pluginTitle
* GET /cpl/download-plugin/:pluginTitle
* GET /cpl/stats
* GET /cpl/stats/:pluginTitle
* POST /cpl/rate/:pluginTitle
* GET /cpl/changelog/:pluginTitle
* GET /cpl/comments/:pluginTitle
* POST /cpl/comments/:pluginTitle
* GET /cpl/comments/pending
* PUT /cpl/comments/:pluginTitle/:commentId
* GET /cpl/compatibility/:pluginTitle
* POST /cpl/compatibility/:pluginTitle
* GET /cpl/compatibility/pending
* PUT /cpl/compatibility/:pluginTitle/:reportId
* GET /cpl/compatibility-related/:pluginTitle
* GET /cpl/auth/status
* GET /cpl/auth/config
* GET /cpl/auth/github/callback?code=...
* POST /cpl/auth/logout
